preamble: The reduction of the voiceless alveolar fricative [s] to the palatal glide [j] (here represented by 'y') is a form of palatalization and lenition. This often occurs in fast speech or as a transitional stage in the evolution of palatalized sibilants.

examples.note: Fast speech reduction or dialectal stages in palatalization.

sources: Ladefoged, P., & Maddieson, I. (1996). The Sounds of the World's Languages.
Alkire, T., & Rosen, C. (2010). Romance Philology.
